codeReview.summary: Feature requirements are substantially implemented: business registration/login now use email+password, legacy login-password endpoint and unauthorized modal are removed, and business login UI is simplified to a single-step form. One non-blocking edge-case remains in approval enforcement when a profile row is missing.

issues.description: Approval gating is only enforced when a profile row exists (`if (profile && profile.is_approved === false)`). If `business_owner_profiles` is missing for an owner, login still succeeds with `profile: null`, which bypasses the expected approval/profile check in this edge case.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `fullstack-worker` skill marks lint as mandatory in automated verification, but this session transcript shows type-check and build only (no `npm run lint`). This can cause inconsistent worker behavior vs. skill expectations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure §4) says `Run npm run lint -- fix any errors`; transcript skeleton for session `a89c07a8-de20-4d10-be4e-c6cf993fd632` includes `npm run type-check` and `npm run build` commands but no `npm run lint`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Agent-browser execution guidance appears underspecified in the worker skill; the worker attempted unsupported CLI forms before succeeding, indicating a discoverability gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session `a89c07a8-de20-4d10-be4e-c6cf993fd632` contains `Unknown command: start` and `Unknown command: --headless` during UI verification attempts.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, and skill guidance for auth-business-simplify. Implementation meets the feature intent and expected behavior overall, with one non-blocking edge-case in login approval enforcement when profile data is missing.
